ehr.primaryDx: Bipolar I disorder, current episode mixed, severe

priorLevelsOfCare.outcome: Discharged after 3 days; re-presented to ED 9 days later with acute decompensation

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: Tell me what's been happening. You were just at the PHP nine days ago.

segments.speaker: patient
segments.text: I know. I thought I could manage but everything fell apart again the same week.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: Are you having any thoughts of hurting yourself or others right now?

segments.speaker: patient
segments.text: Yes. I have a plan. I know what I would use and when. I can't promise I won't do it.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: Can you agree to a safety plan with us and not act on those thoughts?

segments.speaker: patient
segments.text: No. I don't think I can keep that promise. I don't trust myself right now.

segments.speaker: nurse
segments.text: Patient will need one-to-one observation given active plan and inability to contract for safety.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: Has anyone been home with you? Can you describe your living situation?

segments.speaker: patient
segments.text: My roommate moved out. There's no one there. It's been completely chaotic, I haven't slept in three days.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: Are you eating?

segments.speaker: patient
segments.text: Not really. I had coffee today. I can't sit still long enough to eat.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: You were on a PHP nine days ago and decompensated within the week of leaving. That tells us a lower level of care is not going to hold right now.

segments.speaker: patient
segments.text: I know the PHP wasn't enough. I need more support than that.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: We're going to start you on lithium and quetiapine. The lithium will need close monitoring while we titrate to a therapeutic level.

segments.speaker: nurse
segments.text: We will get labs in the morning and titrate based on the lithium level. She will need daily draws until we're in range.

segments.speaker: social_worker
segments.text: Given her living situation and the rapid return after PHP, step-down to outpatient or PHP again would be premature at this time.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: Agreed. We need inpatient until medications are stable and safety can be established more reliably.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: Day three review. Let's go through each domain.

segments.speaker: nurse
segments.text: He remains on one-to-one observation. Overnight he was found attempting to tie a sheet around his neck and was redirected by staff.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: That's a significant incident. Has he spoken about his intentions?

segments.speaker: patient
segments.text: I just want it to stop. I know I tried something last night. I can't guarantee I won't try again.

segments.speaker: nurse
segments.text: He still isn't sleeping. He was awake all night pacing and required two staff to manage behavioral agitation around 3 AM.

segments.speaker: nurse
segments.text: He refused all meals yesterday and needed staff assistance to change clothes this morning.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: What's the lithium level from this morning?

segments.speaker: nurse
segments.text: Zero point four milliequivalents per liter. Still subtherapeutic. We are titrating upward per protocol.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: So we are actively titrating the lithium and the quetiapine dose was increased last night. Neither medication is at therapeutic range yet.

segments.speaker: social_worker
segments.text: He has no safe discharge environment. His roommate is gone, there is no family in the area, and he has no outpatient team currently established.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: The PHP failed nine days before admission. We cannot step him back down before the medications are therapeutic and safety is established.

segments.speaker: care_team
segments.text: We'll continue one-to-one, continue titrating medications, and hold on any discharge planning until the clinical picture stabilizes.

segments.speaker: social_worker
segments.text: He explicitly left the PHP against staff advice on day three of that program, which also contributed to his rapid return.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: Given the self-harm incident last night, the subtherapeutic medication levels, and the lack of any safe environment to return to, continued inpatient care is clearly indicated at this time.

segments.speaker: nurse
segments.text: Agreed. He would not be safe in a less structured setting right now.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: Day four. Any change overnight?

segments.speaker: nurse
segments.text: He slept about three hours, which is an improvement, but he remains hypervigilant and was pacing for most of the night shift.

segments.speaker: nurse
segments.text: He accepted breakfast this morning but only ate about a quarter of the tray. Still required prompting.

segments.speaker: patient
segments.text: I feel a little less like jumping out of my skin today. But I still have those thoughts.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: The lithium level this morning was zero point six. Still not therapeutic. We're continuing the titration.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: He's showing early signs of partial response but persistent acute symptoms remain. We need to continue close monitoring.

segments.speaker: social_worker
segments.text: I've reached out to a community mental health center to establish outpatient care, but nothing is in place yet.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: We will reassess discharge readiness once the lithium is therapeutic and he can demonstrate sustained safety without one-to-one.
